p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot, dense foliage, circular succulent leaves, stable posture.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained consistent throughout the sequence; no significant growth or decline observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy; turgor pressure appears optimal with no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot, two primary wide leaves, central position.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The plant has remained static in size; no new leaf emergence noted.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stable; leaf color remains consistent with no necrotic progression.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot, two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The leaf orientation has shifted slightly due to light tracking, but the overall structure is unchanged.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Moderate; slight browning at the leaf tip suggests minor environmental stress or past moisture inconsistency.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot, small seedling near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Remains in a dormant or very slow growth phase; no morphological changes detected.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Stable; small size makes it vulnerable, but no signs of decay.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently damp across all pots.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or secondary seedlings detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Debris (white particulate matter) has been introduced to the soil surface of p3 and p4 between T-3 and T-2.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Significant change in camera angle/perspective; p3 leaf tip browning became more visible.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_2: Introduction of white granular debris (likely perlite or mineral supplement) onto the soil surface of p3 and p4.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_Current: No further structural changes; the environment appears static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is stable. The primary concern is the localized necrosis on the p3 leaf, which has not progressed further, indicating the stressor has been mitigated.
vision_report.anomalies: The appearance of white granular debris on the soil surface of the black pots (p3/p4) starting at T-2 is a human-induced change, likely an amendment to the soil.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is in a state of stasis. The plants are maintaining their current physiological state without significant growth or decline. The most notable event in the sequence is the addition of soil amendments, which suggests active caretaking. The plants are well-hydrated and show no signs of acute distress.
